report_metadata.cancer_type: Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer (NSCLC) — KRAS G12C Mutant Adenocarcinoma

treatments.name: Pembrolizumab + Carboplatin/Pemetrexed (KEYNOTE-189 Regimen)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 3 RCT (KEYNOTE-189) with robust long-term data. NCCN Category 1 recommendation for first-line metastatic non-squamous NSCLC. Exploratory KRAS subgroup analysis shows OS benefit. Gold-standard evidence level.

survival_benefit.rationale: In KRAS G12C subgroup, median OS not reached vs 8 months (HR 0.28). However, STK11 co-mutation significantly attenuates immunotherapy benefit — only 7% response rate to immunotherapy in KRAS/STK11 co-mutant tumors. For this specific patient, the immunotherapy component may underperform. Overall median OS ~22 months in unselected population.

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved, NCCN Category 1 recommended. Universally available at all oncology centers. Standard insurance coverage. No trial enrollment required. Can start immediately.

safety_profile.rationale: Well-characterized safety profile with years of real-world experience. Grade 3+ AEs in ~50% but manageable with established protocols. Immune-related AEs require monitoring. Myelosuppression from chemotherapy. Good supportive care guidelines exist.

biomarker_match.rationale: Not KRAS-targeted. While KRAS G12C subgroup showed benefit in KEYNOTE-189, STK11 co-mutation strongly predicts poor immunotherapy response (7% ORR). This patient's molecular profile (KRAS G12C + STK11) suggests limited benefit from the immunotherapy component. Chemotherapy backbone provides some benefit regardless.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Pembrolizumab blocks PD-1 on T cells, restoring anti-tumor immune response. Carboplatin induces DNA crosslinks causing cancer cell death. Pemetrexed inhibits thymidylate synthase and other folate-dependent enzymes essential for DNA synthesis. Chemotherapy-induced immunogenic cell death may enhance immune checkpoint blockade efficacy.

treatments.name: Adagrasib (Krazati) + Pembrolizumab (First-Line, KRYSTAL-7)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 2 data mature with median PFS 27.7 months in PD-L1>=50% cohort; Phase 3 randomized trial (KRYSTAL-7) actively recruiting. ORR 59.3% with DOR 26.3 months. Adagrasib individually FDA-approved for 2L NSCLC.

survival_benefit.rationale: Exceptional median PFS of 27.7 months with median DOR of 26.3 months in PD-L1>=50% patients. In STK11-mutant subgroup, adagrasib monotherapy showed ORR 30.3% and DCR 66.7%, demonstrating activity in this difficult-to-treat population where immunotherapy alone fails. Combination with pembrolizumab may further enhance benefit.

accessibility.rationale: Adagrasib is FDA-approved for second-line KRAS G12C NSCLC. The first-line combination with pembrolizumab is available through KRYSTAL-7 Phase 3 trial. Off-label first-line use could be considered. Patient is excellent trial candidate.

safety_profile.rationale: Known toxicities include diarrhea, nausea, vomiting, fatigue, and elevated liver enzymes. Adagrasib has CYP3A4 interactions requiring medication review. Combination with pembrolizumab adds immune-related AE risk. Grade 3+ events manageable with dose modifications.

biomarker_match.rationale: Directly targets KRAS G12C mutation. Demonstrated specific activity in STK11 co-mutant patients (ORR 30.3% as monotherapy), directly relevant to this patient. Adagrasib also shows CNS penetration, important for metastatic disease.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Adagrasib is a covalent KRAS G12C inhibitor that irreversibly binds to the cysteine-12 residue of KRAS G12C in its GDP-bound inactive state, blocking downstream RAS/MAPK signaling. It has a long half-life (~23 hours) and CNS penetration. Combined with pembrolizumab (anti-PD-1 checkpoint inhibitor), the approach simultaneously targets the oncogenic driver and restores anti-tumor immunity.
treatments.availability: Clinical Trial (KRYSTAL-7 Phase 3 recruiting) / Adagrasib FDA-approved for 2L

treatments.name: Sotorasib (Lumakras) Monotherapy (Second-Line)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 3 CodeBreaK 200 trial vs docetaxel. PFS significantly improved (HR 0.66, p=0.002). FDA accelerated approval. CodeBreaK 100 Phase 2 showed 2-year OS of 32.5%. NCCN recommended for second-line.

survival_benefit.rationale: Median PFS 5.6 months vs 4.5 months docetaxel. No OS benefit (10.6 vs 11.3 months, HR 1.01) though confounded by crossover. 2-year OS 32.5% in CodeBreaK 100. Modest PFS improvement in second-line setting. STK11 co-mutant patients showed consistent benefit with sotorasib over docetaxel.

accessibility.rationale: FDA accelerated approval for KRAS G12C-mutated locally advanced/metastatic NSCLC after prior systemic therapy. NCCN recommended. Available at most oncology centers. Oral tablet formulation (960mg daily).

safety_profile.rationale: Fewer grade 3+ TRAEs than docetaxel (33.1% vs 40.4%). Fewer serious AEs (10.7% vs 22.5%). Main toxicities: diarrhea, elevated liver enzymes, nausea. Oral administration preferred by patients. Well-characterized safety profile.

biomarker_match.rationale: Specifically targets KRAS G12C. Sotorasib showed consistent benefit in STK11 co-mutant subgroup. Direct molecular targeting regardless of immune microenvironment status.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Sotorasib is a first-in-class covalent KRAS G12C inhibitor that irreversibly binds to the P2 pocket adjacent to the mutant cysteine-12 residue when KRAS is in the GDP-bound inactive state, trapping it in an inactive conformation and blocking downstream RAF/MEK/ERK signaling.

key_evidence.journal: New England Journal of Medicine / Journal of Clinical Oncology

treatments.name: Adagrasib (Krazati) Monotherapy (Second-Line)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 3 KRYSTAL-12 met primary endpoint: PFS 5.5 vs 3.8 months (HR 0.58, p<0.0001). ORR 32% vs 9%. Also demonstrated intracranial activity (iORR 24% vs 11%). FDA approved for second-line KRAS G12C NSCLC.

survival_benefit.rationale: Median PFS 5.5 months vs 3.8 months for docetaxel. ORR 32% with DOR 8.3 months. OS data immature but numerically favorable. CNS penetration provides benefit for brain metastases. Modest but meaningful improvement over chemotherapy in second-line.

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved for previously treated KRAS G12C-mutated locally advanced or metastatic NSCLC. Oral formulation (400mg BID). Available at major oncology centers. Standard insurance pathways.

safety_profile.rationale: No new safety signals in KRYSTAL-12. Common AEs: diarrhea, nausea, vomiting, fatigue, elevated liver enzymes. CYP3A4 interaction potential requires medication review (relevant for patient's GERD medications). QTc prolongation risk requires monitoring.

biomarker_match.rationale: Specifically targets KRAS G12C. Demonstrated activity in STK11 co-mutant patients (ORR 30.3%, DCR 66.7% as monotherapy in first-line). CNS penetration valuable for metastatic disease management. Long half-life (~23 hours) allows sustained target engagement.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Adagrasib covalently and irreversibly binds to the mutant cysteine-12 of KRAS G12C in its GDP-bound inactive state, preventing GTP loading and downstream MAPK pathway activation. It features a long half-life (~23 hours) for sustained target inhibition and demonstrates blood-brain barrier penetration, providing intracranial activity.

treatments.name: Divarasib (GDC-6036) Monotherapy

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 1 long-term follow-up published in JCO 2025. ORR 53.4% (Phase 1 full cohort) with median PFS 13.8 months overall and 15.3 months at 400mg dose. Phase 2/3 trials ongoing. Well-characterized safety with 48% treated beyond 1 year.

survival_benefit.rationale: Median PFS of 15.3 months at recommended dose is the longest reported for any single-agent KRAS G12C inhibitor, exceeding sotorasib (5.6 mo) and adagrasib (5.5 mo) by a wide margin. Durable responses with 48% of patients treated beyond 1 year.

accessibility.rationale: Not yet FDA approved. Available through ongoing Phase 2/3 clinical trials. Phase 3 trial in NSCLC ongoing. Combination trial with pembrolizumab (NCT05789082) also enrolling.

safety_profile.rationale: Excellent safety profile. No dose-limiting toxicities or treatment-related deaths in Phase 1. Grade 3 events in only 11% of patients. Safety profile consistent beyond 1 year of treatment. Best-in-class tolerability among KRAS G12C inhibitors.

biomarker_match.rationale: Directly targets KRAS G12C mutation. Once-daily oral dosing. Potent and selective next-generation inhibitor with improved binding characteristics compared to first-generation agents.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Divarasib is a next-generation covalent KRAS G12C inhibitor that binds irreversibly to the mutant cysteine-12 residue in the GDP-bound inactive state of KRAS G12C, potently blocking RAS/MAPK signaling. It has enhanced potency and selectivity compared to sotorasib and adagrasib, with once-daily oral dosing and a favorable pharmacokinetic profile.

treatments.name: Olomorasib (LY3537982) + Pembrolizumab (First-Line)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 1b/2 data with FDA Breakthrough Therapy Designation (Sept 2025); registrational Phase 3 SUNRAY-01 trial enrolling. ORR 63% in first-line patients with 78% ORR in small 1L subset (n=9). Strong signal but Phase 3 data pending.

survival_benefit.rationale: Median PFS 11.8 months with ORR 57.1% across all patients; in first-line patients ORR 78% and DCR 100%. Next-generation KRAS G12C inhibitor designed to overcome limitations of sotorasib/adagrasib. Particularly relevant for STK11 co-mutant patients who respond poorly to immunotherapy alone.

accessibility.rationale: Not yet FDA approved; available through SUNRAY-01 Phase 3 trial (NCT06119581) which is actively recruiting. FDA Breakthrough Therapy Designation suggests accelerated pathway. Patient's ECOG 0 and age 45 make excellent trial candidate.

safety_profile.rationale: Diarrhea most common TEAE; 33.4% grade 3+ treatment-related adverse events. Manageable toxicity profile with dose modifications. Combination toxicity requires careful monitoring.

biomarker_match.rationale: Specifically targets KRAS G12C mutation which is confirmed in this patient. Efficacy maintained across PD-L1 expression levels (75% ORR in PD-L1>=50%, 56% in PD-L1<50%), making it suitable regardless of PD-L1 status. Particularly valuable given STK11 co-mutation which limits immunotherapy-only approaches.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Olomorasib is a second-generation covalent KRAS G12C inhibitor that irreversibly locks KRAS G12C in its inactive GDP-bound state with improved potency and selectivity compared to first-generation agents. Combined with pembrolizumab (anti-PD-1), this dual blockade targets both the oncogenic driver and immune evasion simultaneously, addressing the immunosuppressive microenvironment created by KRAS/STK11 co-mutation.
treatments.availability: Clinical Trial (SUNRAY-01, NCT06119581) — FDA Breakthrough Therapy Designation

treatments.name: Glecirasib + JAB-3312 (SHP2 Inhibitor) — First-Line Combination

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 1/2a data showing ORR 72.5% and DCR 96.3% in first-line NSCLC. Phase 3 registrational trial (NCT06416410) actively recruiting comparing to standard of care. Promising preclinical rationale for overcoming adaptive resistance.

survival_benefit.rationale: ORR of 72.5% and DCR of 96.3% in first-line NSCLC patients are among the highest reported for any KRAS G12C combination. SHP2 inhibition addresses key resistance mechanism by blocking RAS pathway reactivation, potentially providing more durable responses.

accessibility.rationale: Available only through clinical trials. Phase 3 trial recruiting but primarily in select global sites. Neither glecirasib nor JAB-3312 are FDA-approved. May be harder to access than other trial options.

safety_profile.rationale: Combined KRAS G12C + SHP2 inhibition has manageable toxicity based on Phase 1/2a data. Expected class effects include GI toxicity and peripheral edema. Full safety profile still being characterized.

biomarker_match.rationale: Specifically targets KRAS G12C. The SHP2 combination rationale is particularly strong because SHP2 activates RAS through dephosphorylation, and blocking this bypass mechanism addresses the most common adaptive resistance pathway to KRAS G12C inhibitors.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Glecirasib is a potent covalent KRAS G12C inhibitor that locks KRAS in its GDP-bound inactive state. JAB-3312 is an allosteric SHP2 phosphatase inhibitor that blocks upstream RAS pathway reactivation through SOS1-mediated nucleotide exchange. The combination prevents adaptive feedback resistance by simultaneously blocking the oncogenic driver and its key reactivation pathway.

treatments.name: Sotorasib (Lumakras) + Carboplatin/Pemetrexed (SCARLET Regimen)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 2 SCARLET trial (WJOG14821L) with 30 patients showed remarkable ORR of 88.9%. Median PFS 6.6 months and median OS 20.6 months. Single-arm design limits evidence level. Sotorasib individually FDA-approved.

survival_benefit.rationale: Median OS 20.6 months with ORR 88.9% is impressive for first-line treatment. However, median PFS was only 6.6 months suggesting rapid resistance. 12-month OS rate of 66.7%. Particularly notable: 100% ORR in PD-L1<1% patients, directly relevant for STK11 co-mutant tumors that typically have low PD-L1.

accessibility.rationale: Sotorasib is FDA-approved (accelerated approval for 2L). Carboplatin and pemetrexed are standard chemotherapy agents. This combination could potentially be used off-label in first-line setting. Study data supports clinical rationale.

safety_profile.rationale: Significant hematological toxicity: anemia, thrombocytopenia, neutropenia as common grade 3+ events. One treatment-related death (pneumonia). Triple-drug combination carries meaningful toxicity burden. Requires growth factor support and close monitoring.

biomarker_match.rationale: Targets KRAS G12C specifically. Particularly relevant for this patient: 100% ORR in PD-L1<1% patients (STK11 co-mutant tumors often have low PD-L1 and cold immune microenvironment). Chemotherapy backbone provides antigen release independent of immune status.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Sotorasib covalently binds KRAS G12C in the GDP-bound state, blocking oncogenic signaling. Carboplatin creates DNA crosslinks causing cell death, while pemetrexed inhibits multiple folate-dependent enzymes critical for DNA synthesis. The triple combination provides simultaneous targeted pathway blockade and cytotoxic cell killing, potentially overcoming immune-cold microenvironments in STK11 co-mutant tumors.

treatments.name: Nivolumab + Ipilimumab + Chemotherapy (CheckMate 9LA Regimen)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 3 CheckMate 9LA demonstrated OS benefit for nivolumab+ipilimumab+limited chemo vs chemo alone. FDA approved first-line. Anti-CTLA-4 (ipilimumab) may partially overcome STK11-mediated immune evasion by activating different T cell compartments.

survival_benefit.rationale: Median OS approximately 15.8 months vs 11.0 months with chemo alone. However, STK11 co-mutation remains a negative prognostic factor even with dual immunotherapy. Some data suggests anti-CTLA-4 may provide incremental benefit in KRAS/STK11 co-mutant tumors compared to anti-PD-1 alone, but evidence is limited.

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved first-line combination for metastatic NSCLC. NCCN recommended. Widely available at oncology centers. All three components commercially available.

safety_profile.rationale: Dual checkpoint inhibitor combination carries higher immune-related AE risk: colitis, hepatitis, pneumonitis, endocrinopathies. Grade 3+ AEs in approximately 47%. Requires experienced immune toxicity management. Limited chemotherapy cycles (2 cycles) reduce chemo-related toxicity.

biomarker_match.rationale: Not KRAS-targeted. Anti-CTLA-4 theoretically provides complementary immune activation to anti-PD-1, potentially partially overcoming STK11-mediated immune resistance. However, STK11 creates a fundamentally cold tumor microenvironment that limits all immunotherapy approaches. Modest theoretical advantage over single-agent checkpoint inhibitor.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Nivolumab (anti-PD-1) restores T cell anti-tumor activity. Ipilimumab (anti-CTLA-4) enhances T cell priming and activation in lymph nodes, expanding the anti-tumor T cell repertoire. Limited-duration chemotherapy (2 cycles) provides early tumor control and immunogenic cell death to prime immune responses. The dual checkpoint approach targets different phases of the immune response.

key_evidence.journal: Journal of Clinical Oncology / Lancet Oncology

treatments.name: JDQ443 Monotherapy (KontRASt Program)
treatments.category: Targeted Therapy (Next-Generation KRAS G12C Inhibitor, Experimental)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 1b/2 data (KontRASt-01) with ORR 54.5% in NSCLC at recommended dose (200mg BID). Phase 3 KontRASt-02 comparing to docetaxel is ongoing. Phase 2 KontRASt-06 specifically targets STK11 co-mutant population.

survival_benefit.rationale: ORR 54.5% in pretreated NSCLC, competitive with other KRAS G12C inhibitors. Also demonstrated activity in prior KRAS G12C inhibitor-treated patients (ORR 33.3% with TNO155 combination). Novel binding mechanism may overcome some resistance mechanisms.

accessibility.rationale: Clinical trial only. KontRASt-06 is specifically designed for STK11 co-mutant patients, making it particularly relevant for this patient. Multiple trials recruiting globally. Not yet FDA approved.

safety_profile.rationale: Well-tolerated in Phase 1b/2. Once-daily dosing at 200mg BID. Safety profile comparable to other KRAS G12C inhibitors. Unique binding mechanism avoids H95 interaction, potentially reducing some resistance-related toxicity issues.

biomarker_match.rationale: Targets KRAS G12C. KontRASt-06 trial specifically enrolls STK11 co-mutant NSCLC patients, making it tailor-made for this patient's molecular profile. Novel binding mechanism (unique spiro-azolide linker bypassing H95) may provide activity after resistance to other KRAS G12C inhibitors.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: JDQ443 is a structurally distinct KRAS G12C inhibitor featuring a 5-methylpyrazole core and spiro-azolide linker that forms novel interactions in the Switch-II pocket while bypassing the H95 residue. This unique binding mode durably locks KRAS G12C in the GDP-bound inactive state and may provide activity in tumors resistant to other KRAS G12C inhibitors.
treatments.availability: Clinical Trial Only (KontRASt-02 Phase 3, KontRASt-06 Phase 2 for STK11 co-mutant)

treatments.name: Docetaxel + Ramucirumab (Second-Line Chemotherapy)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 3 REVEL trial demonstrated OS benefit. Well-established second-line option for NSCLC. NCCN recommended. However, KRAS G12C-specific subgroup analyses show inferior outcomes compared to KRAS G12C inhibitors.

survival_benefit.rationale: In KRAS G12C-mutant patients, real-world median OS only 6.0 months and median PFS 3.4 months. In general NSCLC, median OS 10.5 vs 9.1 months. KRAS-mutant status associated with significantly worse PFS. Clearly inferior to KRAS G12C inhibitors in this molecular subgroup.

accessibility.rationale: Both drugs FDA approved and widely available. Standard insurance coverage. Can be administered at any oncology center. No biomarker requirements for access.

safety_profile.rationale: Significant toxicity: neutropenia, febrile neutropenia, fatigue, stomatitis, hypertension (ramucirumab). Higher grade 3+ AE rate than KRAS G12C inhibitors. Requires frequent IV infusions. Hair loss. Neuropathy risk.

biomarker_match.rationale: Not molecularly targeted. KRAS mutation status does not predict benefit and may predict worse outcomes. Anti-VEGFR2 activity of ramucirumab provides anti-angiogenic benefit regardless of genotype but is not optimized for this patient's driver mutation.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Docetaxel stabilizes microtubules, preventing cell division and inducing apoptosis. Ramucirumab is a monoclonal antibody targeting VEGFR2, inhibiting tumor angiogenesis and vascular supply. The combination provides cytotoxic and anti-angiogenic activity independent of KRAS mutation status.

treatments.name: Garsorasib (D-1553) Monotherapy
treatments.category: Targeted Therapy (Next-Generation KRAS G12C Inhibitor, Experimental)

evidence_level.rationale: Early-phase clinical data available from Phase 1/2 studies. Demonstrated anti-tumor activity in KRAS G12C-mutant NSCLC. Less mature data compared to divarasib, olomorasib, and JDQ443. Phase 2 expansion ongoing.

survival_benefit.rationale: Early-phase data shows promising response rates in KRAS G12C NSCLC, though detailed PFS/OS data is limited. Adds to the armamentarium of next-generation KRAS G12C inhibitors. May provide sequential treatment option after resistance to other KRAS G12C inhibitors.

accessibility.rationale: Available only in early-phase clinical trials with limited geographic availability. Not FDA approved. Primarily available in China-based trials initially, with some global expansion.

safety_profile.rationale: Generally well-tolerated in early-phase studies. Oral once-daily dosing. Common AEs include GI toxicity and liver enzyme elevation, consistent with KRAS G12C inhibitor class effects. Safety profile still being characterized.

biomarker_match.rationale: Directly targets KRAS G12C mutation. May have differentiating structural features that provide activity in tumors resistant to first-generation inhibitors.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Garsorasib (D-1553) is a potent, selective, orally bioavailable covalent KRAS G12C inhibitor that locks the mutant protein in its inactive GDP-bound state. It features a distinct chemical scaffold that may provide differentiated activity compared to other KRAS G12C inhibitors.

clinical_trials.title: SUNRAY-01: First-Line Olomorasib (LY3537982) and Pembrolizumab With or Without Chemotherapy in Patients With Advanced KRAS G12C-Mutant NSCLC
biomarker_requirements: No prior systemic therapy for metastatic disease
clinical_trials.early_results: Phase 1b/2: ORR 63% in first-line patients (n=9, ORR 78%, DCR 100%). FDA Breakthrough Therapy Designation granted September 2025. Median PFS 11.8 months across all patients.

clinical_trials.title: KRYSTAL-7: Phase 3 Study of First-Line Adagrasib Plus Pembrolizumab vs Pembrolizumab Alone in Advanced NSCLC With KRAS G12C Mutation
clinical_trials.early_results: Phase 2: Median PFS 27.7 months with ORR 59.3% and DOR 26.3 months in PD-L1>=50% cohort. Adagrasib monotherapy ORR 30.3% in STK11 co-mutant subgroup.

clinical_trials.title: KontRASt-06: JDQ443 Monotherapy as First-Line Treatment for Advanced NSCLC With KRAS G12C Mutation (Including STK11 Co-Mutation Cohort)
clinical_trials.early_results: KontRASt-01 Phase 1b/2: ORR 54.5% at 200mg BID in NSCLC (n=38). JDQ443+TNO155 ORR 33.3% in prior KRAS G12Ci-treated patients.

clinical_trials.title: Divarasib (GDC-6036) Phase 2/3 in Solid Tumors With KRAS G12C Mutation Including NSCLC
clinical_trials.early_results: Phase 1 long-term follow-up: ORR 53.4%, median PFS 15.3 months at 400mg dose (n=44). 48% treated beyond 1 year. Excellent safety: only 11% grade 3 AEs.

clinical_trials.title: Phase 3 Study of Glecirasib + JAB-3312 (SHP2 Inhibitor) vs Standard of Care in First-Line KRAS G12C NSCLC
clinical_trials.early_results: Phase 1/2a: First-line ORR 72.5%, DCR 96.3% (n=80). One of the highest response rates reported for KRAS G12C combinations.

clinical_trials.title: Divarasib (GDC-6036) in Combination With Pembrolizumab in KRAS G12C-Mutant NSCLC
clinical_trials.early_results: Divarasib monotherapy median PFS 15.3 months; combination with pembrolizumab data maturing.

clinical_trials.title: Combination Therapies With Adagrasib in Patients With Advanced NSCLC With KRAS G12C Mutation
clinical_trials.early_results: Evaluating adagrasib + pembrolizumab + chemotherapy in first-line setting for PD-L1 TPS >=1% and <50%.

combination_strategies.rationale: KRAS G12C inhibition induces tumor cell death and antigen release while remodeling the tumor microenvironment to be more immunologically active. Combined with anti-PD-1, this dual approach targets both the oncogenic driver and immune evasion. Particularly relevant for STK11 co-mutant tumors where KRAS G12C inhibitors may help 'warm up' the cold immune microenvironment. KRYSTAL-7 showed mPFS 27.7 months; olomorasib+pembro ORR 63% in 1L.

SHP2 mediates adaptive resistance to KRAS G12C inhibitors by reactivating RAS through SOS1-mediated nucleotide exchange. Blocking SHP2 simultaneously with KRAS G12C prevents this feedback loop, achieving deeper and more durable responses. First-line ORR 72.5% and DCR 96.3% in Phase 1/2a support the mechanistic rationale.

Chemotherapy provides cytotoxic cell killing independent of immune microenvironment, critical for STK11 co-mutant tumors with cold immune phenotype. Combined with KRAS G12C inhibition, this addresses both the driver oncogene and treatment-resistant tumor subclones. SCARLET trial: ORR 88.9%, and notably 100% ORR in PD-L1<1% patients, directly relevant for STK11 co-mutant tumors.

Novel KRAS G12C inhibitor with unique binding mechanism (bypasses H95) combined with SHP2 inhibition to overcome adaptive resistance. Demonstrated activity even in patients previously treated with other KRAS G12C inhibitors (ORR 33.3%), providing a sequential treatment strategy after resistance to first-generation agents.

SOS1 is the guanine nucleotide exchange factor that reactivates RAS by promoting GDP-to-GTP exchange. Dual blockade of KRAS G12C (locking inactive state) and SOS1 (preventing reactivation) targets resistance at two complementary points in the RAS cycle. Multiple SOS1 inhibitors in early clinical development.

supportive_care.evidence: Multiple randomized clinical trials including JAMA Network Open 2024 publication demonstrate that early palliative care integration improves quality of life, mood, symptom control, and overall survival in advanced NSCLC. ASCO strongly recommends early palliative care for all patients with metastatic NSCLC.
supportive_care.benefit: Improved quality of life, reduced depression, better symptom management, and potential overall survival improvement. Temel et al. landmark study showed 2.7-month OS improvement with early palliative care in metastatic NSCLC.

supportive_care.evidence: Bone metastases occur in up to 60% of NSCLC patients. NCCN guidelines recommend bone-targeted agents for patients with bone metastases. Denosumab may enhance immunotherapy efficacy through immune modulation. Regular monitoring with bone scans recommended.
supportive_care.benefit: Reduction in skeletal-related events (pathologic fractures, spinal cord compression, need for radiation/surgery to bone). Denosumab delayed time to first SRE by 8.2 months vs zoledronic acid in lung cancer subset.

supportive_care.evidence: ASCO 2025 recognizes exercise as medicine for cancer patients. Meta-analyses show exercise during cancer treatment improves physical function, reduces fatigue, and may improve treatment tolerance. Colon cancer RCT (NEJM 2025) showed HR 0.72 for disease-free survival with structured exercise. Lung cancer-specific data emerging but limited for stage IV.
supportive_care.benefit: Improved physical function and ECOG performance status maintenance, reduced cancer-related fatigue, better treatment tolerance, potential survival benefit. Patient's current ECOG 0 makes early exercise intervention particularly important to maintain functional status.

supportive_care.evidence: Malnutrition prevalence in lung cancer patients is 45-69%. ESPEN guidelines recommend nutritional screening and support for all cancer patients. Patient's GERD comorbidity requires management — proton pump inhibitors may interact with sotorasib absorption (gastric pH-dependent). Dietary counseling to manage treatment-related GI side effects (diarrhea, nausea).
supportive_care.benefit: Maintained body weight and muscle mass, improved treatment tolerance, better quality of life. GERD management critical to ensure adequate drug absorption for oral KRAS G12C inhibitors. Consider timing of PPI administration relative to targeted therapy dosing.

supportive_care.evidence: NCCN Distress Management Guidelines recommend routine distress screening for all cancer patients. Young age (45) and family history of cancer are additional psychosocial risk factors. Stage IVB diagnosis in a young patient carries significant psychological burden.
supportive_care.benefit: Reduced anxiety and depression, improved treatment adherence, better quality of life. Evidence shows that addressing psychological distress improves overall outcomes and treatment completion rates.
